segments.text: Once again, Jesus spoke to them in parables:
The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding banquet for his son.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He sent his servants to call those he had invited to the banquet, but they refused to come.
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: Again, he sent other servants and said, ‘Tell those who have been invited that I have prepared my dinner. My oxen and fattened cattle have been killed, and everything is ready. Come to the wedding banquet.’
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: But they paid no attention and went away, one to his field, another to his business.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The rest seized his servants, mistreated them, and killed them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The king was enraged, and he sent his troops to destroy those murderers and burn their city.
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: Then he said to his servants, ‘The wedding banquet is ready, but those I invited were not worthy.
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: Go therefore to the crossroads and invite to the banquet as many as you can find.’
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: So the servants went out into the streets and gathered everyone they could find, both evil and good, and the wedding hall was filled with guests.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But when the king came in to see the guests, he spotted a man who was not dressed in wedding clothes.
segments.speaker: King

segments.text:
‘Friend,’ he asked, ‘how did you get in here without wedding clothes?’
But the man was speechless.
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: Then the king told the servants, ‘Tie him hand and foot, and throw him into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: For many are called, but few are chosen.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the Pharisees went out and conspired to trap Jesus in His words.
They sent their disciples to Him along with the Herodians.
Teacher,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they said,
we know that You are honest and that You teach the way of God in accordance with the truth. You seek favor from no one, because You pay no attention to external appearance.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So tell us what You think: Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar or not?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But Jesus knew their evil intent and said,
You hypocrites, why are you testing Me?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Show Me the coin used for the tax.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And they brought Him a denarius.
Whose image is this,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He asked,
and whose inscription?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Caesar’s,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
they answered.
So Jesus told them,
Give to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And when they heard this, they were amazed. So they left Him and went away.
That same day the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to Jesus and questioned Him.
Teacher,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they said,
Moses declared that if a man dies without having children, his brother is to marry the widow and raise up offspring for him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married and died without having children. So he left his wife to his brother.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers, down to the seventh.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And last of all, the woman died.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In the resurrection, then, whose wife will she be of the seven? For all of them were married to her.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Jesus answered,
You are mistaken because you do not know the Scriptures or the power of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the resurrection, people will neither marry nor be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like the angels in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But concerning the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what God said to you:
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? He is not the God of the dead, but of the living.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When the crowds heard this, they were astonished at His teaching.
And when the Pharisees heard that Jesus had silenced the Sadducees, they themselves gathered together.
One of them, an expert in the law, tested Him with a question:
Teacher, which commandment is the greatest in the Law?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus declared,
‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This is the first and greatest commandment.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And the second is like it: ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All the Law and the Prophets hang on these two commandments.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: While the Pharisees were assembled, Jesus questioned them:
What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is He?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: David’s,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they answered.
Jesus said to them,
How then does David in the Spirit call Him ‘Lord’? For he says:
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘The Lord said to my Lord,“Sit at My right handuntil I put Your enemiesunder Your feet.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So if David calls Him ‘Lord,’ how can He be David’s son?”
No one was able to answer a word, and from that day on no one dared to question Him any further.
